usefulness_evaluation.narrative: This is a structural/adversarial test persona that does not evaluate usefulness. All 10 edge case tests passed: validation interceptor correctly rejects malformed UUIDs, empty/oversized strings; status machine enforces valid transitions (COMPLETED->PLANNING rejected with failed_precondition); DeleteBooking is idempotent (silently succeeds on non-existent ID); ListTrips returns empty pagination object instead of error for zero trips. The API's error handling is robust with no panics, 500s, or incorrect error categories.

test_results.notes: Correct error code and message. No panic or 500.

test_results.actual: invalid_argument: validation error: id: value must be a valid UUID
test_results.notes: Validation interceptor correctly catches malformed UUID before it reaches business logic.

test_results.actual: invalid_argument: validation error: title: value length must be at least 1 characters
invalid_argument: validation error: title: value length must be at most 512 characters

test_results.expected: Error or rejection of backward transition
test_results.actual: failed_precondition: invalid trip status transition: completed -> planning
test_results.notes: Status machine correctly rejects backward transition. Trip remained COMPLETED after rejection. Skipping PLANNING->ACTIVE (directly to COMPLETED) was allowed, which is reasonable for the test scenario.

test_results.actual: invalid_argument: validation error: content: value length must be at least 1 characters
test_results.notes: buf.validate min_len constraint enforced on streaming RPC.

test_results.actual: invalid_argument: validation error: content: value length must be at most 10000 characters

test_results.notes: Idempotent delete - returns success even when booking does not exist. This is acceptable REST/RPC design.

test_results.actual: invalid_argument: validation error: raw_text: value length must be at least 1 characters
Success: {"pagination":{}} (empty response with pagination object, no trips array)
test_results.notes: Returns successful response with empty pagination. The trips field is omitted (proto3 default for empty repeated fields) rather than an explicit empty array, which is standard proto3 behavior.
